audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Sabbath grainfields, Sabbath healing, choosing the Twelve, plain sermon blessings/woes, enemy-love, mercy, judging, tree/fruit, and foundation sayings.

verses.restored: On a Sabbath he was passing through grainfields, and his disciples were plucking and eating heads of grain, rubbing them in their hands.
Some of the Pharisees said, Why are you doing what is not lawful on the Sabbath?
Jesus answered them, Have you not read what David did when he was hungry, he and those with him,
how he entered the house of the Divine, took the bread of the Presence, ate, and gave to those with him, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests alone?
He said to them, The Son of Humanity is lord of the Sabbath.
On another Sabbath, he entered the synagogue and taught. A man was there whose right hand was withered.
The scribes and Pharisees were watching him closely, whether he would heal on the Sabbath, so that they might find an accusation against him.
He knew their reasonings and said to the man with the withered hand, Rise and stand in the middle. He rose and stood.
Jesus said to them, I ask you: Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good or to do harm, to save life/soul or to destroy?
Looking around at them all, he said to him, Stretch out your hand. He did, and his hand was restored.
They were filled with mindless fury and discussed with one another what they might do to Jesus.
In these days he went out to the mountain to pray, and he spent the night in prayer to the Divine.
When day came, he called his disciples and chose from them twelve, whom he also named apostles/sent ones.
Simon, whom he also named Peter, Andrew his brother, James, John, Philip, Bartholomew,
Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Simon called Zealot,
Judas son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who became a betrayer.
Coming down with them, he stood on a level place, with a great crowd of his disciples and a great multitude of people from all Judea, Jerusalem, and the coastal region of Tyre and Sidon.
They came to hear him and to be healed from their diseases; those troubled by unclean spirits were healed.
All the crowd sought to touch him, because power was going out from him and healing all.
Lifting up his eyes to his disciples, he said: Blessed/flourishing are you poor, because yours is the reign of the Divine.
Blessed/flourishing are you who hunger now, because you will be filled. Blessed/flourishing are you who weep now, because you will laugh.
Blessed/flourishing are you when people hate you, exclude you, insult you, and reject your name as harmful because of the Son of Humanity.
Rejoice in that day and leap, for look, your reward is great in heaven; for their fathers did likewise to the prophets.
But woe to you rich, because you have received your consolation.
Woe to you who are full now, because you will hunger. Woe to you who laugh now, because you will mourn and weep.
Woe when all people speak beautifully of you, for their fathers did likewise to the false prophets.
But to you who hear I say: love your enemies, do beautiful/good to those who hate you,
bless those who curse you, pray concerning those who mistreat you.
To the one striking you on the cheek, offer also the other; from the one taking your cloak, do not withhold the tunic also.
Give to everyone asking you, and from the one taking what is yours, do not demand it back.
Just as you want people to do to you, do likewise to them.
If you love those loving you, what grace is that to you? For even sinners/misaligned ones love those loving them.
If you do good to those doing good to you, what grace is that to you? Even sinners/misaligned ones do the same.
If you lend to those from whom you hope to receive, what grace is that to you? Even sinners/misaligned ones lend to sinners/misaligned ones to receive back the same.
But love your enemies, do good, and lend, expecting nothing back; your reward will be great, and you will be sons of the Most High, because he is kind to the ungrateful and harmful.
Become merciful, just as your Father is merciful.
Do not judge, and you will certainly not be judged; do not condemn, and you will certainly not be condemned; release, and you will be released.
Give, and it will be given to you: good measure, pressed down, shaken together, running over, they will give into your lap; for with the measure you measure, it will be measured back to you.
He also spoke a parable to them: Can a blind person guide a blind person? Will not both fall into a pit?
A disciple is not above the teacher, but everyone fully formed will be like the teacher.
Why do you look at the speck in your brother's eye, but do not notice the beam in your own eye?

How are you able to say to your brother, Brother, let me take out the speck in your eye, while you yourself do not see the beam in your eye? Hypocrite, first take the beam out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take out the speck in your brother's eye.
For there is no beautiful/good tree making rotten fruit, nor again a rotten tree making beautiful/good fruit.
Each tree is known from its own fruit; for people do not gather figs from thorns, nor do they harvest grapes from a bramble bush.
The good person from the good treasure of the heart brings out good, and the harmful person from harmful treasure brings out harm; for from the abundance of the heart the mouth speaks.
Why do you call me Lord, Lord, and not do what I say?
Everyone coming to me, hearing my words, and doing them, I will show you whom that one is like.

That one is like a person building a house, who dug deep and set foundation on rock. Flood came, the river broke against that house and was not strong enough to shake it, because it had been well built.
The one hearing and not doing is like a person building a house on the earth without foundation; the river broke against it, and immediately it fell, and the ruin of that house was great.
